mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of configuration for distributed setups.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for vLLM to serve a model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample vLLM resource to serve the 'gpt-3' model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o yaml

```

> This command retrieves the details of the vLLM resource to verify its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM instances.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o yaml > backup-example-vllm.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o yaml

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to version 0.18.0.
> Verify the status of the upgraded vLLM resource.
commands: kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o yaml > backup-example-vllm.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the vLLM chart is installed and check the namespace with 'kubectl get namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create vLLM instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model may not be available or the configuration is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the model name and configuration in the Custom Resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits, and ensure the cluster has sufficient capacity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management with PagedAttention.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource with status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get vllm'.

kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements for running vLLM.
